     The Numbers Business Report

How to Be a Latin Lover (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $32,149,404Details
International Box Office $30,406,824
Worldwide Box Office $62,556,228
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $920,724
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $707,774
Total $1,628,498
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Handsome and very young Maximo has a dream to be rich without ever having to work a day in his life. Having made a career of seducing rich older women, he marries a wealthy woman more than twice his age. 25 years later, spoiled and bored from waking up next to his now 80-year-old wife, he gets the surprise of his life when she ends up dumping him for a younger car salesman. Forced out of his mansion and desperate for a place to stay, he must move in with his estranged sister, Sara, and her nerdy but adorable son, Hugo, in their small apartment. Anxious to return to the lap of luxury, Maximo uses his nephew’s crush on a classmate to get to his new target—her grandmother, Celeste, a widowed billionaire. As Maximo tries to rekindle his powers as a Latin lover, he finds himself bonding with his nephew Hugo, and he begins to learn that being a Latin lover means that loving money isn't as important as the love of your family.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,252,439 (38.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.62 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$10,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,118 opening theaters/1,203 max. theaters, 4.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $42,865,872

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 28th, 2017 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: May 5th, 2017 (Wide), released as Cómo Ser Un Latin Lover (Mexico)
May 5th, 2017 (Wide), released as Как да бъдеш латино любовник (Bulgaria)
June 30th, 2017 (Wide) (Brazil)
July 14th, 2017 (Wide), released as Milovník po přechodu (Czech Republic)
July 14th, 2017 (Wide) (Slovakia)
Video Release: August 1st, 2017 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for crude humor, sexual references and gestures, and for brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2463 (Cert #50876), 2/15/2017)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut
Plot point: Bad Role Models, Dysfunctional Family, Faked Romance, May / December Romance, Relationships Gone Wrong
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Target audience: Hispanic
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 3Pas Studios, Pantelion Films, Lionsgate, Videocine
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Spanish

Synopsis

Handsome and very young Maximo has a dream to be rich without ever having to work a day in his life. Having made a career of seducing rich older women, he marries a wealthy woman more than twice his age. 25 years later, spoiled and bored from waking up next to his now 80-year-old wife, he gets the surprise of his life when she ends up dumping him for a younger car salesman. Forced out of his mansion and desperate for a place to stay, he must move in with his estranged sister, Sara, and her nerdy but adorable son, Hugo, in their small apartment. Anxious to return to the lap of luxury, Maximo uses his nephew’s crush on a classmate to get to his new target—her grandmother, Celeste, a widowed billionaire. As Maximo tries to rekindle his powers as a Latin lover, he finds himself bonding with his nephew Hugo, and he begins to learn that being a Latin lover means that loving money isn't as important as the love of your family.
